doc.summary: This plugin extends Contact Form 7 by adding dynamic form fields that accept shortcodes to prepopulate form fields with default values and dynamic placeholders.
features: Adds dynamic text, hidden, email, URL, tel, number, range, textarea, select, checkbox, radio, date, submit, and label fields to CF7.
Allows fields to be prepopulated with dynamic values via shortcodes (e.g., from GET, POST, URL, user data, post meta, etc.).
Supports dynamic placeholders and values.
Provides AJAX handling for dynamic evaluation after page load.
Registers and enqueues a frontend JavaScript asset for dynamic functionality.
Adds custom form tags and validation filters to Contact Form 7.
Supports deprecated CF7 tags (e.g., 'text', 'hidden') with dynamic extensions.
notes: Requires Contact Form 7 plugin to be active.
Minimum required CF7 version for mail validation is 5.7.
Minimum required CF7 version for tag generation is 6.0.
The plugin includes logic to check for and enqueue its frontend script conditionally.
It includes support for loading custom shortcodes from the active theme or site root.
The file starts with a PHP opening tag and includes a comment stating 'Silence is golden' as a general description.

doc.summary: A JavaScript object (dtx) designed to handle dynamic text extension values for Contact Form 7 (CF7) fields on the client side. It initializes by scanning input fields, resolves values (either immediately or by queuing for AJAX), and sets the values on the respective input elements.
features: Initializes by finding inputs with class 'dtx-pageload' and 'data-dtx-value'.
Supports various CF7 shortcode types (e.g., 'CF7_GET', 'CF7_referrer', 'CF7_URL', 'CF7_get_cookie', 'CF7_guid', 'CF7_get_current_var', etc.).
Handles immediate value resolution for certain shortcodes (e.g., 'CF7_get_cookie', 'CF7_guid').
Queues requests for AJAX resolution for other shortcodes.
Performs an asynchronous AJAX call to a specified endpoint ('wpcf7dtx') to fetch queued values.
Provides helper methods like `validKey` to check attribute existence and type.
Includes an `obfuscate` method to convert values (e.g., to HTML entities).
Sets the resolved value on the input field using `dtx.set`.
Generates a cryptographically secure GUID if available, otherwise falls back to a time/random-based method.
notes: The script assumes jQuery is available and uses `jQuery.noConflict()` if it's already in use.
The AJAX call is wrapped in a 10ms `setTimeout` to ensure asynchronous execution.
The script relies on a global `dtx_obj` object (not defined in the snippet) to provide `ajax_url` and `n` (likely a nonce).
It checks for `window.crypto` availability for robust GUID generation.

doc.summary: A JavaScript utility object (dtx) for handling data transfer and initialization on page load, likely for Contact Form 7 integration. It reads data from input fields, queues it, and then sends it via AJAX to a WordPress backend for processing and setting values on the form fields.
features: Reads data attributes from input fields with class 'dtx-pageload'.
Parses data attributes (which can contain multiple key=value pairs).
Provides methods to retrieve various types of data (e.g., CF7 shortcodes, referrer, current URL, cookies, GUID).
Supports data obfuscation using a configurable method.
Queues data retrieval requests and executes them via AJAX upon page load.
Updates the input field's value, adds 'dtx-loaded' class, and triggers 'dtx_init' event upon successful AJAX response.
notes: Relies on jQuery ($) and assumes a global `dtx_obj` object exists to provide `ajax_url` and `n` (nonce/identifier).
The `init` function runs immediately and queues requests if any fields are found.
The AJAX call uses POST method, sends data as JSON, and sets `cache: false`.
The `current_url` function can extract specific parts of the URL (scheme, host, port, path, query, fragment).
The `guid` function generates a UUID, falling back to a standard implementation if `window.crypto` is unavailable.

doc.summary: A JavaScript module that extends jQuery functionality to manage and update form tag generation options, specifically for WPForms/Contact Form 7 (CF7) dynamic tags.
features: Adds 'dtx-form-tag' class to specific DTX buttons for CSS styling.
Adds 'button button-secondary' class to the close dialog button in the tag generator.
Provides a `replaceAll` helper function to replace all occurrences of a substring within a string.
Provides an `updateOption` function to encode the current input value, replace single quotes with HTML entities, and update a hidden input field's value, triggering a 'change' event.
notes: The module is wrapped in an Immediately Invoked Function Expression (IIFE) that accepts jQuery as an argument.
The `replaceAll` function handles regex creation, allowing for global replacement and optional escaping of special characters in the search string.
The `updateOption` function uses `encodeURIComponent` on the value after running it through `replaceAll` to ensure proper encoding for HTML attributes.

doc.summary: This file provides a collection of shortcode functions for the Contact Form 7 Dynamic Text Extension (DTX) plugin, allowing users to dynamically insert various pieces of data into their forms.
features: Shortcode initialization via the 'init' hook.
Retrieves variables from GET and POST requests.
Fetches current URL or specific parts of the URL (scheme, host, path, query).
Retrieves the referring URL.
Gets various blog information (e.g., site name, title).
Retrieves post variables (title, ID, slug) from a specified post.
Fetches custom fields (post meta) from a specified post.
Gets variables from the current object (user, post, term, archive, search/404).
Retrieves data from the currently logged-in user.
Retrieves attachment IDs or URLs based on ID, size, and post ID.
Gets the value of a specified cookie.
Retrieves taxonomy terms (names, slugs, IDs) for a post.
Fetches theme modification options.
Generates a random GUID.
notes: All shortcode functions utilize various WordPress filters ('wpcf7dtx_shortcode', 'wpcf7dtx_escape', 'wpcf7dtx_sanitize') for data processing.

Many functions rely on helper functions like 'wpcf7dtx_array_has_key', 'wpcf7dtx_validate_post_id', 'wpcf7dtx_post_meta_key_access_is_allowed', and 'wpcf7dtx_access_denied_alert' (though these helpers are not defined in this snippet, their usage implies their existence).
The 'wpcf7dtx_get_current_var' function handles complex logic to determine the context (user, post, term, archive) before retrieving the requested variable.

doc.summary: This plugin adds custom validation messages, extends available error codes, and implements frontend and backend validation for Contact Form 7 Dynamic Text Extension (DTX) fields. It specifically checks for required fields, data types (email, tel, number, date), length constraints, and validates sensitive data access within form tags and placeholders.
features: Adds custom validation messages for DTX fields (e.g., invalid email, phone, number, date).
Extends the list of available error codes in Contact Form 7.
Performs frontend validation on form tags, handling single and multiple selections.
Validates field values based on their defined type (email, tel, number, date).
Checks for required fields and length constraints (min/max) during validation.
Performs backend validation on form tags used in mail configuration.
Validates sensitive data access within form tag values (default/hangover) and placeholders using shortcode parsing.
Handles specific email validation checks for mail configuration (e.g., invalid mailbox syntax, domain checks).
notes: Requires Contact Form 7 (CF7) to be active.
The validation logic is heavily integrated with CF7's validation lifecycle.
The plugin supports both deprecated ('dynamic_') and modern DTX form tags.
Backend validation is triggered via the 'wpcf7_config_validator_validate' hook.
Sensitive data validation relies on helper functions like 'wpcf7dtx_post_meta_key_access_is_allowed' and 'wpcf7dtx_user_data_access_is_allowed' (which are assumed to exist elsewhere).

doc.summary: This class, CF7DTX_Plugin_Settings, manages the configuration and settings page for the Contact Form 7 Dynamic Text Extension plugin. It handles registering settings, displaying fields and sections, and provides functionality to scan forms for dynamic text keys (Post Meta and User Data).
features: Registers settings under the 'cf7dtx_settings' group.
Provides a dedicated admin menu page under the 'wpcf7' menu.
Allows configuration of settings via fields (textarea, select, etc.) and sections.
Implements a form scanning mechanism to detect dynamic text keys used in CF7 forms.
Handles saving settings, including adding newly scanned keys to the respective allow lists.
Displays scan results, including warnings if more forms need scanning or success messages if all keys are covered.
notes: The plugin relies on global constants like 'WPCF7DTX_DATA_ACCESS_KB_URL'.
The scanning process is paginated using `num_forms_to_scan` (default 20) and offset parameters.
The settings page supports various field types: text, checkbox, textarea, select, password, wysiwyg, email, url, color, date.
The scan results page provides a mechanism to add selected keys to the allow lists via a form submission.

doc.summary: Provides functionality to check for plugin updates, scan form data access keys for required allow-listing, and display an administrative notice when intervention is needed.
features: Checks plugin version against database version on `plugins_loaded`.
Updates database version if source code is newer.
Triggers update handlers on `admin_init` if an update is available.
Scans forms (up to 20 by default) to detect keys needing allow-listing.
Sets scan status ('intervention_required', 'intervention_not_required', etc.) in the database.
Displays an admin notice if the scan status requires intervention, with links to settings and scanning.
notes: The scan logic defaults to checking 20 forms, but alerts regardless if more than 20 forms exist.
The notice is suppressed on the 'cf7dtx_settings' screen if specific query parameters are present.
The notice is only shown to users with the 'administrator' role.
